questions.text: A chemical process in which a substance reacts with oxygen to give off heat is called:
questions.explanation: Combustion is the scientific name for burning.

questions.text: The substances which give off heat and light during combustion are called:
questions.explanation: Fuels can be solid, liquid, or gas.

questions.text: Which of the following is essential for combustion?
questions.explanation: Oxygen (air) is a supporter of combustion.

questions.text: The lowest temperature at which a substance catches fire is called its:
questions.explanation: A substance cannot catch fire as long as its temperature is lower than its ignition temperature.

questions.text: Which of these is a non-combustible substance?
questions.explanation: Iron does not burn in air under normal conditions.

questions.text: Substances which have very low ignition temperature and can easily catch fire with a flame are called:
questions.explanation: Examples include petrol, alcohol, and LPG.

questions.text: A fire caused by oil or petrol should be extinguished using:
questions.explanation: Water is heavier than oil and will sink, allowing oil to continue burning on top.

questions.text: The most common fire extinguisher for electrical equipment is:
questions.explanation: CO2 does not conduct electricity and doesn't damage sensitive equipment.

questions.text: The type of combustion in which a substance bursts into flames without any apparent cause is:
questions.explanation: Forest fires or coal dust fires are examples of spontaneous combustion.

questions.text: The combustion of crackers is an example of:
questions.explanation: Explosion involves sudden reaction with heat, light, and sound.

questions.text: Which zone of a candle flame is the hottest?
questions.explanation: The outer zone undergoes complete combustion and is the hottest part.

questions.text: The luminous part of the candle flame is the:
questions.explanation: The middle zone produces light due to incomplete combustion of carbon particles.

questions.text: Goldsmiths use which part of the flame for melting gold and silver?
questions.explanation: They blow the outermost zone because it provides the highest temperature.

questions.text: The amount of heat energy produced on complete combustion of 1 kg of a fuel is called its:
questions.explanation: Calorific value is expressed in kilojoule per kg (kJ/kg).

questions.text: Which of the following has the highest calorific value?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen has a calorific value of 1,50,000 kJ/kg.

questions.text: Incomplete combustion of fuels produces a very poisonous gas called:
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide (CO) can be fatal if inhaled in a closed room.

questions.text: Increased concentration of carbon dioxide in the air is believed to cause:
questions.explanation: CO2 is a greenhouse gas that traps heat in the atmosphere.

questions.text: Oxides of sulphur and nitrogen dissolve in rain water to form:
questions.explanation: Acid rain is harmful to crops, buildings, and soil.

questions.text: An ideal fuel should have ________ ignition temperature.
questions.explanation: It should be above room temperature but easy to reach for usage.

questions.text: The 'black' innermost zone of a candle flame is due to:
questions.explanation: This zone is cool and contains vapours of the wax that haven't burnt yet.

questions.text: Fire is produced by a combination of:
questions.explanation: Removing any of these three requirements will extinguish the fire.

questions.text: Which of these is used to extinguish fires involving electrical wiring?
questions.explanation: Dry powder and CO2 are non-conductors.

questions.text: The head of a safety match contains:
questions.explanation: The rubbing surface contains red phosphorus, which converts to white and ignites the head.

questions.text: When a person's clothes catch fire, the person is covered with a blanket to:
questions.explanation: Smothering the fire with a blanket prevents oxygen from reaching the combustible material.

questions.text: Burning of wood and coal causes ________ of air.
questions.explanation: They release unburnt carbon particles and gases like CO and CO2.

questions.text: Which of the following fuels has the lowest calorific value?
questions.explanation: Cow dung cake has a calorific value of 6000-8000 kJ/kg.

questions.text: Combustion is a ________ reaction.
questions.explanation: It is a chemical reaction that releases energy in the form of heat.

questions.text: Sun produces heat and light by:
questions.explanation: Heat and light in the sun are produced by nuclear fusion, not chemical combustion.

questions.text: If 4.5 kg of a fuel is completely burnt and produces 1,80,000 kJ of heat, what is its calorific value?
The middle zone of a candle flame is ________ in color.
questions.explanation: The yellow color comes from the glow of unburnt carbon particles.

questions.text: LPG is stored in cylinders in ________ state.
questions.explanation: It is liquefied under pressure for storage.

questions.text: In a fire triangle, what happens if heat is removed?
questions.explanation: Removing heat lowers the temperature below the ignition point, stopping the fire.

questions.text: Which of these is not a fossil fuel used for combustion?
questions.explanation: Biogas is a renewable fuel obtained from waste, not a fossil fuel.

questions.text: Rusting of iron is a form of:
questions.explanation: It is a very slow chemical reaction with oxygen that releases heat at an imperceptible rate.

questions.text: A good fuel is one which is:
questions.explanation: These are all characteristics of an ideal fuel.

questions.text: Matchstick starts burning only when rubbed on the side of the box due to:
questions.explanation: Friction produces the initial heat required to reach the ignition temperature.

questions.text: Forest fires are often caused by:
questions.explanation: Multiple factors can trigger forest fires, especially in dry seasons.

questions.text: The partial combustion in the middle zone produces ________.
questions.explanation: Incomplete combustion leaves unburnt carbon particles.

questions.text: Global warming affects:
questions.explanation: Rising average temperatures have global environmental consequences.

questions.text: Water cannot be used to put out a fire in:
questions.explanation: Water is a conductor of electricity and can cause electric shocks to the extinguisher.
